notes: Raises June bike frequency and sustained-power workload after the athlete set 300 W by September 1, 2026 as an explicit stretch target. Uses weekday rides to cover cottage no-bike weekends. Most sessions are training, not tests; validation appears only when it informs the next FTP step.

workouts.title: Tempo Progression Ride
workouts.objective: Add a controlled bike day tomorrow and build sustained aerobic pressure before Thursday threshold.
workouts.why_now: The athlete is not tired, Monday was very easy, and the week needs more bike work. This adds useful tempo without stealing the main threshold session.

targets.notes: 75 minutes with 3 x 10 min tempo at 82-88% FTP. RPE cap 6. Finish wanting Thursday.

steps.notes: Tempo progression. Smooth, repeatable, no threshold creep.

nutrition.pre: Normal meal 2-4h before. If hungry, add 20-30g carbs 30-60 min before.
nutrition.during: Water to thirst; add 20-30g carbs if starting hungry or if the ride is late.
nutrition.post: 25-35g protein plus a normal carb serving at the next meal.
nutrition.centr_recipe: No new Centr link needed; use the cookbook normal recovery plate fallback.
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: none required. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.post_session: Normal meal and fluids. No lower-body strength after.
recovery.evening: Protect sleep; Wednesday stays easy and Thursday is the real threshold evidence.
recovery.next_morning_check: Wednesday proceeds if legs are normal and the tempo did not feel forced.

skill_focus.action: Hold tempo by pressure, not by chasing speed.
skill_focus.why: This shows whether sustained power is repeatable outdoors without turning the day into a threshold race.

skill_focus.action: Rebuild power gradually after corners over 5-8 pedal strokes.
skill_focus.why: Smooth rebuilds make FTP usable on real roads without wasting energy in surges.

mental_focus.why: Tempo volume helps the 300 W target only if Thursday remains high-quality.
mental_focus.cue: Tempo builds the engine; threshold sharpens it.
mental_focus.reset: If power drifts above 88% FTP, shift easier for one minute and restart the block under the cap.

execution_notes.if_too_easy: Hold the cap. The progression is Thursday, not today.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Make the third rep endurance at 58-70% FTP.
execution_notes.success_criteria: All three tempo reps feel controlled and Thursday still looks attractive.

workouts.title: Threshold Progression
workouts.objective: Use repeatable threshold pressure as the week's main FTP-development session.
workouts.why_now: The athlete wants a real path toward 300 W, and recent easy execution supports a stricter target than the previous conservative version.

targets.notes: 90 minutes with 3 x 12 min at 92-98% FTP. RPE cap 8. This is progression work, not a maximal FTP test.

pre_interval_cues: Start only if legs and sleep are normal.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Hold the cap and finish clean; do not turn it into a maximal test.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Drop to 90-94% FTP and complete all three reps.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Third rep remains controlled with no fade and RPE no higher than 8.

workouts.title: Friday Long Aerobic Anchor
workouts.objective: Make Friday the true no-cottage-bike weekend anchor.
workouts.why_now: The athlete wants more bike load and will not have reliable bike access at the cottage. Friday has to carry the weekend endurance dose.

targets.notes: 2h30 endurance durability at 58-74% FTP. No tempo finish after Thursday threshold.

steps.notes: Long aerobic durability. Fuel early, cap climbs, stay below tempo.

nutrition.pre: Carb-forward meal 2-4h before with about 1-2g carbs/kg.
nutrition.during: Start in the first 30 minutes. Target 50-70g carbs/hour and drink to thirst.
nutrition.post: 25-35g protein plus 80-110g carbs across the next meal window.
nutrition.centr_recipe: High-protein Lentil Braise with Flatbread, Centr collection link: https://centr.com/blog/show/32824/10-high-protein-recipes-for-easy-meal-prep
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: electrolytes/sodium if hot or heavy sweating; otherwise none required. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

execution_notes.if_too_easy: Finish steady; do not add tempo after Thursday.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Steady power, no depletion, no need to force Saturday.

workouts.title: Monday Aerobic Build Ride
workouts.objective: Start the next build week with enough volume to avoid drifting fresh.
workouts.why_now: June needs more bike frequency and weekend bike access is unreliable.

workouts.title: Tuesday Aerobic Volume
workouts.objective: Add weekday bike volume so the week no longer depends on cottage riding.
workouts.why_now: The athlete wants more riding, and this is the low-risk place to add it before Wednesday quality.

targets.notes: 75 minutes endurance at 55-70% FTP with light cadence work only.

workouts.title: Sweet Spot Progression
workouts.objective: Build repeatable sub-threshold power with more total pressure than the old version.
workouts.why_now: This creates a second FTP-adjacent training stimulus after the June 11 threshold progression.

targets.notes: 90 minutes with 3 x 15 min at 88-94% FTP. RPE cap 7.

execution_notes.if_too_easy: Hold the cap. Do not turn sweet spot into threshold.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Drop to 86-90% FTP and complete all three reps.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Rep 3 is steady, controlled, and not a fade.

workouts.title: Thursday Easy Support
workouts.objective: Add frequency without adding fatigue before the Friday anchor.
workouts.why_now: The plan needs more riding, but the day before Friday should support the anchor rather than compete with it.

targets.notes: 60 minutes easy endurance at 55-68% FTP. No tempo.

workouts.title: Friday Sustained Power Anchor
workouts.objective: Move the week-endurance dose into Friday with sustained tempo plus long aerobic support.
workouts.why_now: The cottage weekend cannot be assumed rideable, so Friday carries the main durability and sustained-power work.

targets.notes: 2h30 with 2 x 30 min tempo at 80-86% FTP, then endurance. Fuel early.

workouts.title: Tuesday Aerobic Volume
workouts.objective: Keep overload week from depending on weekend access.
workouts.why_now: This adds controlled volume between Monday aerobic work and Wednesday over-under validation.

targets.notes: 75 minutes endurance at 55-70% FTP. No tempo.

workouts.title: Over-Under Progression
workouts.objective: Build FTP control with longer over-under blocks around threshold.
workouts.why_now: The 300 W target needs stronger threshold control, not more conservative holding patterns.

targets.notes: 95 minutes with 4 x 10 min at 94-102% FTP. RPE cap 8. No sprinting the overs.

execution_notes.if_too_easy: Keep the cap. The goal is repeatability, not max watts.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Ride the remaining blocks steady at 90-96% FTP.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Fourth block stays controlled with no fade or panic surges.

workouts.title: Thursday Easy Support
workouts.objective: Add low-cost volume before the long Friday anchor.
workouts.why_now: The overload week needs more bike time, but Friday remains the bigger sustained-power day.

targets.notes: 60 minutes easy endurance at 55-68% FTP. No tempo.

workouts.title: Friday Overload Anchor
workouts.objective: Make Friday the main overload ride of late June.
workouts.why_now: With no reliable cottage weekend bike access, this is the correct place for the biggest sustained-power and durability dose.

targets.notes: 3h with 2 x 40 min tempo at 80-86% FTP plus endurance. Fuel from the first 30 minutes.

steps.notes: Endurance extension. Fuel and cap climbs.

nutrition.pre: Carb-forward meal 2-4h before with about 1-2g carbs/kg.
nutrition.during: Start in first 30 minutes. Target 60-80g carbs/hour if tolerated and drink to thirst.
nutrition.post: 25-35g protein plus 100-130g carbs across the next meal window.
nutrition.centr_recipe: Creamy Roasted Tofu & Cauliflower Korma, Centr collection link: https://centr.com/blog/show/32824/10-high-protein-recipes-for-easy-meal-prep
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: electrolytes/sodium if hot or heavy sweating; optional caffeine only if tolerated and not late. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

execution_notes.if_too_easy: Finish the duration; do not lift above 86% FTP.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Shorten the second tempo block to 25-30 minutes and keep the rest endurance.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Second tempo block is steady, fueled, and not forced.
